Digital Marketing Dashboard

ROLLER — Digital Marketing
Last Updated: 08 Apr 2026, 10:19 AM




Reporting Notes
  • Data sources: GA4 (website traffic), Google Search Console (organic impressions/clicks), Google AdWords (paid search cost/clicks/impressions), Salesforce (MQL, S2, Closed Won pipeline data), HubSpot (lead source attribution, UTM parameters, drill-down fields).
  • Attribution models: First touch uses Original Marketing Channel from HubSpot. Last touch uses Channel Mapped / Latest Marketing Channel from the most recent interaction record. The Pipeline Breakdown tab is fixed to last-touch attribution only.
  • Channel mapping: Channels are resolved as Direct, Paid Search, Organic, Display, Social, Referral, Email, and AI Search. Display is identified via cm360 UTM source. AI Search includes traffic from ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Claude. Social includes LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Reddit, and Outbrain.
  • Pipeline Breakdown coverage: Vertical, Sub-Vertical, and Segment data may not be populated for all MQLs, as these fields are typically enriched later in the lead lifecycle. Rows with unknown values are excluded from breakdown tables. Segment for S2 records is sourced from the Pipeline dataset via Lead ID lookup.
  • DQ Rate: Calculated as the number of disqualified MQLs divided by total MQLs for the selected period and filters. DQ Reason and Sub-Reason are sourced from the MQL dataset.

Striking Distance Opportunities

DQ Analysis

DQ Rate by Channel

DQ Sub-Reasons

Attribution Analysis

Compares first-touch (introducer) vs last-touch (closer) attribution to reveal which channels introduce leads into the funnel and which ones convert them. Also shows page-level content roles.

Channel Role Comparison

How MQL and S2 credit shifts between first-touch and last-touch attribution. A positive shift means the channel gets more credit as a closer; negative means it's stronger as an introducer.

Cross-Channel Flow (MQL)

Shows how MQLs transition from their first-touch channel to their last-touch channel. Read rows as "introduced by" and columns as "converted by". Diagonal cells = same channel for both touches.

Top Channel Pathways

The most common first-touch → last-touch channel combinations, ranked by volume. Highlights which introducer/closer pairings drive the most pipeline.

Page Path Attribution

Compares pages as entry points (first page seen → MQL) vs conversion points (last page seen → MQL). Shows which content introduces vs converts leads across the entire date range.

Incident Report

Notable events, anomalies, and data caveats that may impact reporting accuracy or interpretation.

Channel Incident Description Dates Impacted
Direct Traffic surge from AI Crawlers The recent spike in our GA4 Direct traffic source is for Help Centre articles. The traffic surge is not user growth but instead it's driven by automated crawler activity from AI/search indexing. All from Dallas. This type of activity is typically temporary and occurs in waves, rather than representing ongoing demand. February – March 2026
